description.en: How tropical cyclones are named, retired, and communicated across regions.
description.zh: 热带气旋如何命名、退役以及跨区域传播预警信息。

content.en:
Typhoon names are maintained by regional meteorological committees so warnings stay clear across countries and languages.

Core rules

  • Names come from rotating regional lists.
  • Names may be retired after severe impacts.
  • Name-based alerts should be paired with local official warnings.
content.zh:
台风名称通常由区域气象委员会维护，目的是让跨国家、跨语言的预警沟通更清晰。

核心规则

  • 名称来自轮换区域清单。
  • 造成重大影响的名称可能被退役。
  • 名称信息应与当地官方预警结合使用。

title.zh: 台风/飓风/气旋分级标准

description.en: A practical guide to tropical cyclone wind scales and impact categories.
description.zh: 热带气旋风速等级、影响分类与读图方式。

content.en:
Tropical cyclone classification converts sustained wind into risk language that emergency teams and the public can act on.

What differs by basin

  • The Atlantic commonly uses the Saffir-Simpson hurricane wind scale.
  • Western Pacific agencies use typhoon intensity terms and local warning signals.
  • Impact depends on rainfall, storm surge, terrain, and exposure, not wind alone.
content.zh:
热带气旋分级把持续风速转化为公众和应急部门可理解的风险语言。

不同海区的差异

  • 大西洋常用萨菲尔-辛普森飓风等级。
  • 西北太平洋会结合台风强度术语和本地预警信号。
  • 影响不只取决于风速，还与降雨、风暴潮、地形和暴露度有关。

description.en: How major typhoon records are reviewed and compared across seasons.
description.zh: 重大台风记录如何整理、比较与复盘。

content.en:
Historical typhoon archives help compare landfall risk, rainfall extremes, surge damage, and recovery lessons.

How to read an archive

  • Track intensity together with landfall timing and population exposure.
  • Separate meteorological strength from social impact.
  • Use archives for planning, not for assuming a future storm will repeat exactly.
content.zh:
历史台风档案有助于比较登陆风险、极端降雨、风暴潮损失和恢复经验。

阅读档案的方法

  • 把强度、登陆时间和人口暴露度一起看。
  • 区分气象强度与社会影响。
  • 档案适合风险规划，不代表未来风暴会完全重复。

articles.asset_source: Wikimedia Commons El Niño regional impacts map

description.en: How Pacific sea-surface warming changes rainfall, monsoon, and seasonal weather risks.
description.zh: 太平洋海温异常偏暖如何影响降雨、季风与季节性天气风险。

content.en:
El Niño is a recurring warming of the central and eastern tropical Pacific. It shifts atmospheric circulation and can change weather risks far beyond the Pacific basin.

Signals to track

  • Niño 3.4 sea-surface temperature anomalies.
  • Trade wind weakening and convection moving eastward.
  • Regional rainfall and temperature departures.
content.zh:
厄尔尼诺是中东太平洋热带海域周期性偏暖现象，会改变大气环流，并把天气风险影响扩展到太平洋以外地区。

重点信号

  • Niño 3.4 区域海温距平。
  • 信风减弱以及对流区东移。
  • 各地区降雨和气温相对常年的偏离。

articles.asset_source: Wikimedia Commons ENSO regional impacts map

description.en: The cool phase of ENSO and its influence on seasonal rainfall and storms.
description.zh: ENSO 冷位相如何影响季节降雨、风暴路径和区域风险。

content.en:
La Niña is the cool phase of ENSO, usually linked to stronger Pacific trade winds and shifted rainfall patterns.

Planning notes

  • Effects vary by region and season.
  • It can alter tropical cyclone formation zones.
  • Local forecasts remain necessary for day-to-day decisions.
content.zh:
拉尼娜是 ENSO 的冷位相，通常与太平洋信风增强和降雨带移动有关。

规划提示

  • 影响会随地区和季节变化。
  • 可能改变热带气旋生成区域。
  • 日常决策仍需要查看本地预报。

articles.asset_source: Wikimedia Commons ENSO regional impacts map

description.en: A clear explanation of El Niño, La Niña, neutral phases, and global teleconnections.
description.zh: 解释厄尔尼诺、拉尼娜、中性位相以及全球遥相关。

content.en:
ENSO links ocean temperature, winds, and tropical rainfall into a repeating but irregular climate pattern.

Three phases

  • El Niño: warmer central and eastern Pacific.
  • La Niña: cooler central and eastern Pacific.
  • Neutral: neither warm nor cool thresholds persist.
content.zh:
ENSO 把海温、风场和热带降雨连接成一种重复但不规则的气候振荡。

三种位相

  • 厄尔尼诺：中东太平洋偏暖。
  • 拉尼娜：中东太平洋偏冷。
  • 中性：冷暖信号都未持续达到阈值。

articles.asset_source: Wikimedia Commons global temperature anomaly chart

description.en: A practical weather-data lens on warming, heavy rainfall, drought, and urban risk.
description.zh: 从天气数据角度理解升温、强降雨、干旱与城市风险。

content.en:
Climate change data combines long-term observations with near-real-time weather monitoring. WeatherIndex treats climate context as background information while city pages remain driven by live forecast APIs.

Data points to watch

  • Temperature anomalies against a historical baseline.
  • Heat-wave frequency and duration.
  • Extreme rainfall intensity and drought persistence.
content.zh:
气候变化数据把长期观测与近实时天气监测结合起来。WeatherIndex 将气候内容作为背景知识，城市页面仍以实时预报 API 为核心。

重点数据

  • 相对历史基准的气温距平。
  • 热浪频率和持续时间。
  • 极端降雨强度与干旱持续性。

articles.asset_source: Wikimedia Commons global temperature anomaly chart

description.en: The science behind greenhouse gases, warming trends, and weather extremes.
description.zh: 温室气体、升温趋势与极端天气之间的科学联系。

content.en:
Global warming describes the long-term rise in Earth's average temperature, driven mainly by increasing greenhouse gases.

Why weather users care

  • A warmer atmosphere can hold more water vapor.
  • Heat extremes become more likely in many regions.
  • Baseline climate shifts change how forecasts are interpreted.
content.zh:
全球变暖指地球平均温度长期上升，主要由温室气体浓度增加驱动。

天气用户为什么要关心

  • 更暖的大气可容纳更多水汽。
  • 许多地区高温极端事件概率上升。
  • 气候基线变化会影响我们解读预报的方式。

title.zh: 冰川融化与海平面上升

description.en: How ice loss and ocean warming raise coastal weather and flood risks.
description.zh: 冰川消融和海洋增暖如何提高沿海天气与洪涝风险。

content.en:
Sea level rise is shaped by melting land ice and thermal expansion of seawater. It turns ordinary coastal storms into higher-impact flood events.

Key signals

  • Satellite altimetry tracks global mean sea level.
  • Local land motion can amplify or reduce relative rise.
  • Storm surge risk grows when baseline water levels rise.
content.zh:
海平面上升受陆地冰融化和海水热膨胀共同影响，会让普通沿海风暴造成更高影响的洪涝事件。

关键信号

  • 卫星测高追踪全球平均海平面。
  • 本地地面升降会改变相对海平面变化。
  • 基线水位上升会放大风暴潮风险。

description.en: How carbon-neutral plans connect emissions, energy systems, and climate risk.
description.zh: 碳中和目标如何连接排放、能源系统与气候风险。

content.en:
Carbon neutrality means balancing remaining emissions with removals or verified offsets, while reducing emissions as much as possible first.

What to compare

  • Target year and legal status.
  • Power, transport, buildings, and industry pathways.
  • Whether plans include adaptation for unavoidable weather risk.
content.zh:
碳中和指在尽可能减排之后，通过移除或可信抵消来平衡剩余排放。

比较重点

  • 目标年份和法律约束力。
  • 电力、交通、建筑和工业路径。
  • 是否包含应对不可避免天气风险的适应计划。

description.en: A monthly framework for reviewing global heat, flood, storm, drought, and cold events.
description.zh: 用于每月复盘全球高温、洪水、风暴、干旱和寒潮事件。

content.en:
Extreme weather archives organize events by hazard, location, exposure, and confirmed impacts.

Review checklist

  • Separate forecast signals from verified observations.
  • Track human exposure and infrastructure vulnerability.
  • Compare events with local historical percentiles.
content.zh:
极端天气档案按灾害类型、地点、暴露度和已确认影响来整理事件。

复盘清单

  • 区分预报信号和已验证观测。
  • 记录人口暴露和基础设施脆弱性。
  • 与本地历史百分位进行比较。

description.en: Why prolonged heat and cold happen, and how they affect health and infrastructure.
description.zh: 持续高温和低温如何形成，以及对健康和基础设施的影响。

content.en:
Heat waves and cold waves are sustained temperature extremes relative to local climate, not just single hot or cold days.

Risk factors

  • Duration often matters as much as peak temperature.
  • Nighttime temperatures affect health recovery.
  • Wind, humidity, snow, and power demand change impacts.
content.zh:
热浪和寒潮是相对本地气候而言持续出现的温度极端，并不只是某一天特别热或特别冷。

风险因素

  • 持续时间常常和峰值温度一样重要。
  • 夜间温度影响人体恢复。
  • 风、湿度、积雪和用电需求会改变影响。

description.en: How instability, wind shear, and supercells create severe convective weather.
description.zh: 不稳定能量、风切变和超级单体如何造成强对流天气。

content.en:
Tornadoes are most often linked to severe thunderstorms where instability and wind shear organize rotating updrafts.

Ingredients

  • Warm, moist low-level air.
  • Cooler air aloft and strong instability.
  • Changing wind speed or direction with height.
content.zh:
龙卷风通常与强雷暴有关，当不稳定能量和风切变组织出旋转上升气流时风险升高。

形成条件

  • 低层暖湿空气。
  • 高空较冷空气和强不稳定能量。
  • 风速或风向随高度明显变化。

title.zh: 二十四节气与气象科学

description.en: How traditional seasonal markers relate to modern weather and climate observation.
description.zh: 传统节气如何与现代天气和气候观测相互印证。

content.en:
The 24 solar terms divide the year by the Sun's apparent motion and preserve long-running seasonal knowledge.

Modern reading

  • Solar terms describe seasonal tendency, not exact local weather.
  • Latitude and terrain change how each term feels.
  • Forecast data turns cultural timing into practical planning.
content.zh:
二十四节气根据太阳周年视运动划分一年，保存了长期季节经验。

现代解读

  • 节气描述季节趋势，不等于精确本地天气。
  • 纬度和地形会改变节气体感。
  • 预报数据能把文化时间转化为实际安排。

description.en: Why cities stay warmer and what reduces heat stress at street level.
description.zh: 城市为什么更热，以及街区尺度如何降低热压力。

content.en:
Urban heat islands form when buildings, roads, and limited vegetation store heat and slow nighttime cooling.

Mitigation tools

  • Shade trees and connected green space.
  • Cool roofs and reflective paving.
  • Heat-health alerts tied to local vulnerability.
content.zh:
城市热岛来自建筑、道路和植被不足导致的蓄热，以及夜间降温变慢。

缓解工具

  • 遮荫树木和连续绿地。
  • 冷屋顶和高反射铺装。
  • 结合本地脆弱性的高温健康预警。

description.en: From surface observations and satellites to numerical models and AI-assisted forecasts.
description.zh: 从地面观测、卫星云图到数值模式和 AI 辅助预报。

content.en:
Modern forecasting blends observations, physics-based numerical models, human expertise, and increasingly AI-assisted pattern recognition.

Core pipeline

  • Observe the atmosphere with stations, radar, satellites, and aircraft.
  • Assimilate data into model initial conditions.
  • Compare ensembles to express uncertainty.
content.zh:
现代天气预报结合观测、物理数值模式、预报员经验，以及越来越多的 AI 辅助模式识别。

核心流程

  • 通过气象站、雷达、卫星和飞机观测大气。
  • 把数据同化为模式初始场。
  • 比较集合预报来表达不确定性。
